title: Spicy Chewy Russian Bread Hash
description: A hearty breakfast hash that turns stale rye bread into a caramelized, spicy delight with smoky paprika and chili flakes, finished with fresh parsley for a Mediterranean touch.
instructions: Preheat a large skillet over medium heat and add the olive oil.
Add the minced garlic and diced onion, cooking until the onion becomes translucent and the garlic is fragrant, about 3‑4 minutes.
Stir in the cubed rye bread, ensuring each cube is coated with oil, garlic, and onion.
Add the smoked paprika, red chili flakes, salt, and black pepper, tossing gently to distribute the spices evenly.
Cover the skillet and let the bread toast for 8–10 minutes, stirring occasionally until the edges start to crisp and the interior caramelizes.
Remove the lid and increase the heat to high, letting the hash fry for an additional 2–3 minutes to achieve a smoky char on the bread pieces.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley over the hash and give one final gentle stir.
Serve hot, optionally garnishing with a squeeze of lemon juice for a bright Mediterranean finish.
Enjoy the chewy texture and spicy kick that warms you from the inside out.
